reasoning: The Baa corporate bond spread recently peaked near 1.93 in mid-April 2025 and has subsequently shown significant downward momentum, dropping rapidly from 1.88 in late May to 1.73 by June 6. Financial commentary suggests that although spreads are tight and could widen on macro uncertainty over the medium-term, the immediate trajectory points to continued narrowing as part of recent market optimism. Because of the strong short-term downward momentum (falling 11 basis points in a week), the probability that the spread will be strictly higher than the June 8 baseline by next week is assessed safely below 0.5. Over longer horizons (September and December), standard random walk dynamics and higher forecast uncertainty dictate that the probabilities regress toward 0.5, as both continued compression towards historical floors (e.g. 1.40s) or mean-reverting widening are viable fundamental scenarios. As instructed, predictions regress closer to 0.5 for more remote resolution dates, directly reflecting rising time-based uncertainty.
